content:
Why your phone model changes everything and how much extra screen time you actually get

3 min read
Just now

--

Pretty much everybody uses Dark Mode now. It looks cool, prevents your screen from glaring at you when you’re in a dark place, and the most important reason is that it supposedly saves lots of battery. However, if you switch on Dark Mode hoping that your phone will automatically work for an additional 4–5 hours because of this, then you will most likely be disappointed. The reality is that Dark Mode helps preserve battery, but not every iPhone can benefit from it. Here is the reason why.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Source: Author’s iPhoneActual secret is in the screen
To understand how Dark Mode works, you have to know how your screen lights up. Apple uses two completely different types of screens for their phones. One type saves a lot of battery in Dark Mode, and the other type does not save any at all.
The Flashlight Screen (Older iPhones)
If you have an iPhone 11, iPhone XR, iPhone 8, or an iPhone SE, your phone uses a standard screen (often called an LCD). Think of this screen like a piece of stained glass with a giant flashlight shining behind it. To show you a picture, the flashlight turns on and pushes light…
